title: Simondon's Concretization and the Design of Autonomous Vehicles
venue: Techné: Research in Philosophy and Technology
abstract_snippet: This paper applies Simondon's concept of concretization to the design trajectory of autonomous vehicle systems, arguing that functional convergence in AV architectures exemplifies technical individuation.

title: Phenomenology of Recommendation Systems: When Algorithms Suggest
venue: Techné: Research in Philosophy and Technology
abstract_snippet: Drawing on Ihde's instrumental realism and Verbeek's postphenomenology, this article examines how recommendation algorithms mediate human experience of choice and preference.

title: Infrastructure Ethics: Toward a Philosophy of Maintenance
venue: Techné: Research in Philosophy and Technology
abstract_snippet: We argue for an infrastructure ethics grounded in care and maintenance rather than innovation, drawing on Star's infrastructure studies and feminist philosophy of technology.
